blocks.content: Now that you've selected an authentication method, set your region, and configured the Assume Role ARN, save the data source and verify that Grafana can reach AWS.

Click **Save & test** at the bottom of the **Settings** tab.

Grafana assumes the IAM role and makes a test request to CloudWatch. On success, you'll see a confirmation message that the data source is working.

### Common connection issues

| Symptom | Likely cause | Solution |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| `Access denied` or `not authorized to perform: sts:AssumeRole` | Trust relationship or external ID is wrong | Verify the role trusts the Grafana account ID and requires the exact External ID from the instructions box |
| `User is not authorized to perform: cloudwatch:ListMetrics` | IAM policy is missing required actions | Attach the metrics/logs permissions policy from the earlier milestone to the role |
| `The security token included in the request is invalid` | Role ARN is mistyped | Re-copy the ARN from the AWS Console and paste it into **Assume Role ARN** |
| No data but no error | Wrong region | Confirm the **Default Region** matches where your metrics and logs live |

To troubleshoot further, refer to [Troubleshoot CloudWatch issues](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/aws-cloudwatch/troubleshooting/).
In the next milestone, you'll run a query and explore your CloudWatch data in Grafana.
